Priority:

[ACT] Identify the parent iframe element
[ASSERT] Verify iframe src domain is testflex.cybersource.com
[ACT] Execute in DevTools console: document.querySelector('iframe').contentDocument
Business_Rule:
[DOM] Card number input is inside a cross-origin iframe
[DOM] iframe src domain is testflex.cybersource.com
[STATE] Attempting contentDocument access throws DOMException: Blocked a frame with origin from accessing a cross-origin frame
Edge_Case_Refs:
[NETWORK] No card number field value transmitted to {{BACK_URL}} in any request
[CONSOLE] No iframe access errors other than expected cross-origin DOMException
Preconditions: iframe rendered without cross-origin isolation; contentDocument access succeeds; card field rendered in parent DOM instead of iframe

Title: CVV Iframe Isolation

[ACT] Execute in DevTools console: attempt JavaScript access to CVV iframe contentDocument
Failure_Signals:
[DOM] CVV field is hosted inside a cross-origin iframe
[DOM] iframe has appropriate sandbox attributes
[STATE] JavaScript access to CVV iframe contentDocument is blocked (DOMException)
[STATE] Cannot read or write CVV value from parent page context
Cleanup:
[NETWORK] CVV value does not appear in any network request to {{BACK_URL}}
[CONSOLE] No sandbox or iframe configuration errors
References: CVV field accessible from parent page JavaScript; sandbox attributes missing; CVV value appears in network requests to VC servers

Title: CVV Visual Masking
Assertions:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} cart page
[ACT] Focus CVV/security code field inside CyberSource iframe
[ACT] Type CVV: 838
[ASSERT] Observe the field display — verify digits are masked
[ASSERT] Verify no digits are visible in plain text
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[DOM] CVV field displays masking characters (• or *) instead of actual digits
[STATE] Entered digits 838 are never visible as plain text in the field
[STATE] Shoulder-surfing attack is mitigated by visual masking
Failure_Signals: [CONSOLE] No CVV value logged to browser console
Cleanup: CVV digits visible as plain text; masking not applied; actual digits briefly flicker before masking

Title: JWT Token Authentication

[STATE] Token is not reused across sessions

[CONSOLE] No JWT value logged in plain text to browser console

Title: user={{USER_EMAIL}};password={{USER_PASSWORD}}
Priority:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and log in with {{USER_EMAIL}} / {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[ACT] Open DevTools Application tab — inspect Cookies, localStorage, sessionStorage
[ASSERT] Identify access token storage location and verify HttpOnly flag on sensitive cookies
[ASSERT] Check token expiration (exp claim if JWT, or cookie Max-Age/Expires)
[ACT] Remain idle past token expiry or trigger a refresh action
[ASSERT] Verify refresh mechanism obtains a new token without requiring full re-login
Business_Rule:
[STATE] Sensitive auth tokens stored in HttpOnly cookies (not accessible via document.cookie)
[STATE] Token has a reasonable expiration (not set to never expire)
[STATE] Token refresh mechanism works — new token issued before expiry
[CONSOLE] No token values logged to browser console in plain text
Edge_Case_Refs:
[NETWORK] Token refresh request sent to {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token or equivalent
[CONSOLE] No token values visible in console output
Preconditions: Token stored in non-HttpOnly cookie accessible by JavaScript; token has no expiry; refresh fails silently; token value logged to console

Title: Logout Token Revocation
Assertions:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and log in with {{USER_EMAIL}} / {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[ACT] Open DevTools Network tab — note current access token from request headers or cookies
[ACT] Click logout button/link
[WAIT] Wait for logout to complete and redirect
[ASSERT] Verify a token revocation request was sent to {{BACK_URL}}/connect/revocation or equivalent
[ACT] Attempt to use the captured old token in a direct API request to {{BACK_URL}}
[ASSERT] Verify old token is rejected (401 Unauthorized)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] Logout triggers a POST to token revocation endpoint
[STATE] Old access token returns 401 when used after logout
[STATE] Session cookies are cleared after logout
[NAV] After logout, attempting to access protected pages redirects to login
Failure_Signals:
[API] Direct request to {{BACK_URL}}/api/platform/security with old token returns 401
[NETWORK] Revocation endpoint call present in Network tab after logout
Cleanup: Old token accepted after logout; no revocation request sent on logout; session cookies persist with valid token after logout

Title: Account Lockout Protection

[ASSERT] Verify account is still locked even with correct credentials
[ASSERT] Verify lockout message indicates a timeout or account unlock procedure
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[STATE] Account lockout message appears after 5 consecutive failed attempts
[STATE] Correct password is rejected during lockout period
[STATE] Lockout message is user-friendly (no stack trace or internal info)
[STATE] Lockout has a defined timeout or unlock mechanism
Failure_Signals:

[NETWORK] Login requests after lockout return 401/403 without exposing internal info
Cleanup: No lockout after 5 failures; lockout message not displayed; correct password accepted during lockout; error message exposes internal stack trace or account details

Title: XSS Prevention - Search
Assertions:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Click the search field
[ACT] Type the XSS payload: <script>alert('XSS')</script>
[ACT] Submit the search (press Enter or click search button)
[WAIT] Wait for search results page to load
[ASSERT] Verify no JavaScript alert dialog appears
[ASSERT] Verify search results or empty results page renders without script execution
[ASSERT] Inspect DOM — verify the payload is HTML-escaped in the output
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[STATE] No alert() dialog appears after submitting XSS payload
[DOM] Payload is rendered as escaped HTML (e.g., <script>) not as executable script
[STATE] Search results page loads normally without script injection
Failure_Signals:
[CONSOLE] No alert() or script execution events in browser console — verify zero user script executions
[NETWORK] XSS payload is sanitized before being sent to {{BACK_URL}} search API
Cleanup: JavaScript alert dialog appears; script executes in browser context; DOM renders unescaped <script> tag; browser console shows script execution

Title: Path Traversal Prevention
Preconditions: - Application with URL parameters or file upload accessible
Assertions:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and identify URL parameters that accept file names or paths
[ACT] Modify a URL parameter to include path traversal payload: ../../../etc/passwd
[ACT] If file upload available: attempt to upload a file named ../../../etc/passwd
[WAIT] Wait for the application response
[ASSERT] Verify the request is rejected or returns a safe error
[ASSERT] Verify no file contents from the server filesystem are returned
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[STATE] Path traversal attempt is blocked — 400, 403, or 404 response
[STATE] No sensitive file content (e.g., /etc/passwd contents) returned in response
[STATE] Application returns a safe, generic error message — no internal path details
Failure_Signals:
[NETWORK] Response from {{BACK_URL}} for traversal attempt returns 400/403/404 — no 200 with file contents
[CONSOLE] No internal file path or server filesystem structure in browser console
Cleanup: Server returns file contents from traversal path; internal server path exposed in error message; traversal payload processed without sanitization

Business_Rule:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and open a form (e.g., login, address, or profile form)
[ACT] Open DevTools Network tab
[ACT] Submit the form normally and inspect the request payload
[ASSERT] Verify a CSRF token is included in the form submission (hidden field or request header)
[ACT] Attempt to forge the same request without the CSRF token (via DevTools or curl)
[ASSERT] Verify the server rejects the forged request
Edge_Case_Refs:
[STATE] CSRF token is present in form submissions (as hidden field or X-CSRF-Token header)
[NETWORK] Server rejects forged requests missing the CSRF token with 400 or 403
[STATE] Legitimate form submission with valid CSRF token succeeds normally
Preconditions:
[NETWORK] POST requests to {{BACK_URL}} include CSRF token in headers or body
[API] Forged request to {{BACK_URL}} without CSRF token returns 400/403
Test_Data: CSRF token absent from form submissions; server accepts forged requests without token; CSRF token not validated server-side

Title: Session expiry during checkout — re-auth preserves cart
Preconditions:
- Customer logged in with items in cart
- Checkout flow started (shipping address entered)
- Token expiry mechanism available
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and log in with {{USER_EMAIL}} / {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[ACT] Add one or more products to cart
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/checkout and enter shipping address
[ACT] Force session expiry: open DevTools Application tab and delete auth token cookie, or wait for natural token expiry
[ACT] Attempt to proceed to the payment step
[WAIT] Wait for the system response to the expired session
[ASSERT] Verify re-authentication prompt or redirect to login is shown
[ACT] Re-authenticate with {{USER_EMAIL}} / {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[NAV] Navigate back to {{FRONT_URL}}/checkout
[ASSERT] Verify cart contents are intact (same items, quantities, prices)
[ASSERT] Verify checkout can resume from the shipping/payment step without re-entering address
Assertions:
[STATE] Expired session is detected when attempting a checkout action
[STATE] Re-authentication prompt or login redirect is displayed to the user
[STATE] After re-auth, cart contains the same items, quantities, and prices as before expiry
[STATE] Checkout step is preserved — user does not restart from the beginning
[STATE] No data loss during the session expiry and re-authentication cycle
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] xAPI cart query to {{BACK_URL}}/xapi/cart confirms all original line items and quantities are preserved after re-auth
[CONSOLE] No unhandled errors or stack traces during re-auth flow
[NETWORK] Re-authentication request to {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token succeeds with 200
Failure_Signals: Cart items lost after re-authentication; checkout restarted from the beginning after re-auth; session expiry not detected (silently fails); no re-auth prompt shown

Title: Session re-auth during checkout — resume from last step
Preconditions:
- Customer logged in with items in cart
- Checkout flow in progress (shipping address and method selected)
- Token expiry mechanism available
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and log in with {{USER_EMAIL}} / {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[ACT] Add products to cart
[NAV] Proceed through checkout: enter shipping address and select shipping method
[NAV] Arrive at the payment step
[ACT] Force session expiry: clear auth token from DevTools Application tab (Cookies or localStorage)
[ACT] Attempt to interact with the payment form or submit payment
[WAIT] Wait for the re-authentication prompt to appear
[ASSERT] Verify re-authentication prompt is displayed
[ACT] Re-authenticate with {{USER_EMAIL}} / {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[ASSERT] Verify checkout state: shipping address is still populated
[ASSERT] Verify shipping method selection is preserved
[ASSERT] Verify payment step is accessible without restarting checkout from the beginning
[ACT] Complete the order successfully after re-auth
Assertions:
[STATE] Re-authentication prompt appears when session expires on the payment step
[STATE] Shipping address is still populated after re-auth (not cleared)
[STATE] Shipping method selection is preserved after re-auth
[NAV] User lands on payment step after re-auth — not restarted to checkout beginning
[STATE] Order can be completed successfully after session re-authentication
[STATE] Full checkout state is preserved across the session boundary
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] xAPI cart query to {{BACK_URL}}/xapi/cart confirms cart and shipping method are intact after re-auth
[NETWORK] Re-auth token request returns 200 and new valid token is issued
[CONSOLE] No unhandled errors during re-auth checkout resume
Failure_Signals: Shipping address cleared after re-auth; checkout restarts from beginning; shipping method selection lost; payment step inaccessible after re-auth; order creation fails after re-auth

Title: Session expiry does not create partial or orphan order
Preconditions:
- Customer logged in with items in cart
- Checkout flow started with all required information entered
- xAPI access available for order verification
- Token expiry mechanism available
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and log in with {{USER_EMAIL}} / {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[ACT] Add products to cart and proceed through all checkout steps (address, shipping, payment details)
[ACT] Force session expiry immediately before order submission: clear auth token in DevTools Application tab
[ACT] Attempt to submit/place the order with the expired token
[WAIT] Wait for the application response
[ASSERT] Verify order submission fails with a clear, visible error (not silently)
[ACT] Re-authenticate with {{USER_EMAIL}} / {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[ACT] Execute xAPI orders query: { orders(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}", cultureName: "en-US") { totalCount items { number status createdDate } } }
[ASSERT] Verify no new partial or orphan order exists for the failed submission
[ASSERT] Verify cart still contains all original items
[ACT] Complete the order normally after re-auth
[ASSERT] Verify final order is created successfully and has a valid order number
Assertions:
[STATE] Order submission with expired token fails with a clear error message visible to the user (not a silent failure)
[STATE] No partial or orphan order exists in the system after the failed submission
[STATE] No payment was captured or authorized for the failed submission attempt
[STATE] Cart remains intact with all original items after session expiry
[STATE] Order completes normally after re-authentication
[STATE] System maintains transactional integrity — no order record created until full successful completion
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] xAPI query { orders(...) { totalCount items { number status } } } to {{BACK_URL}}/xapi confirms zero new orders after failed attempt
[NETWORK] Failed order submission returns 401 — not 200 or 500
[CONSOLE] No unhandled promise rejections or silent errors when order submission fails with expired token
Failure_Signals: Silent failure on order submission with expired token; partial order record created in system; payment charged or authorized for failed attempt; cart items lost after session expiry; duplicate order created after re-auth and retry
